reasoning: Butterfly is a rigid property, as it cannot change without losing its identity (it is the same species). It provides its own identity conditions for its instances.

It carries identity conditions (i.g., an object is red if and only if it cannot be another color) but does not supply its own. It is anti-unity as it cannot be a single type of entity itself, and it is dependent on a specific external entity (the object or surface) to apply to.

Food is rigid because an instance of a food type cannot cease to be that type without losing its identity (it doesn't change into another type). It carries identity conditions inherited from its parent types but does not supply a unique identity condition of its own.

Animal is a rigid property, as an instance cannot cease to be an animal without losing its identity (it doesn't change into another kind). It provides its own identity conditions for its instances and are the basis for a common unity criterion.

RedApple is a rigid property, as it cannot change without losing its identity (it is not the same as an apple that is not red). It supplies its own identity conditions for distinguishing it from other apples.

A Group is anti-rigid because its members can change, it carries identity but does not supply its own, it is non-unifying as it does not represent a single single entity, and it is dependent on its constituent members.

Country is a rigid property because its identity (e.g., France) remains the same throughout time. It supplies its own identity conditions (internal boundaries, population, government etc.) and is a single, einheit, organic whole.
